metadata.title:
\. P. TRAIN
MEN ARE


STILL OUT

SOME RETURN TO WORK ON


THE GREAT NORTHERN.

pages.raw_text:
There is nothing stirring among
the idle members of the Brother
hood of Railroad Trainmen on the
lines of the Northern Pacific this
morning, despite the fact that re-
ports from all up and down the
Great Northern have it that they
are returning to work in masses
there. 1t is doubtful If they will
return to work on the Northern
Pacific until settlement or COM
promise of the troubles existing be-
tween the officials of the road and
the SwitchrIen ems union has been eff
fected.


At was reported yesterday, the
order for the B. of r. T. men to re-
turn to work under penalty of being
expelled from the organization only
applies to roads on which their
union holds a majority over the
strikers. Members of the Switch
mems union declare they cannot
tell why the railroads think their
part of the strike is practically at
an end. for they have been given
no late information of any attempt





proffered advance of 2 cents an
hour, which has been spurned.


Much discussion is heard among
members of both the Switchmen's
union and the r. of II. II. over the
report that the railroads crippled
by the strike are to hire strike
breakers at the Denver" scale of
wages.


This scale, the strikers declare,
is 10 cents an hour in excess of the
wages that were being paid to
them just before they began their
strike, and four cents an hour more
they they are now demanding.


All the railroads involved in the
strike are being crowded hard for
service by the merchants and ship
pers all over the country. Three
through freight trains were run
west through Spokane over the
Northern Pacific last night and
one went east.
